execution_method: docker exec -e TIMEOUT_BIN=timeout -e NEXUS_ROOT=/Users/ghost/core/nexus -e HEXA_BIN=/usr/local/bin/hexa hexa-exec bash /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool/bridge_health.sh
container_image: hexa-runner:latest (sha256:16830cbffaeb27a0cc21c4a807b7caad3cf3b35a7d23450838650e915174b183) — built this session via hexa-lang fe20e3dc; tag ab8bec13 alias

measurement_setup_notes.first_attempt_ec_126_for_all_16: Initial in-container run with default HEXA_BIN=$HOME/core/hexa-lang/hexa returned ec=126 across all 16 bridges, masquerading as 0 PASS. Root cause: the default $HEXA_BIN is the resolver shim which delegates to /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa.real — that is a Mac (Darwin/arm64) ELF mounted into a linux/amd64 container ('cannot execute binary file: Exec format error'). HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 (already set inside bridge_health.sh) prevents the docker re-route safety net from saving us — in-container we must point at the linux-native binary baked in at /usr/local/bin/hexa.
measurement_setup_notes.fix_applied: override HEXA_BIN=/usr/local/bin/hexa via docker exec -e (no script edit, no image mutation, no git change — measurement-only, per task constraints).
measurement_setup_notes.host_side_comparison: Re-ran bash /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool/bridge_health.sh on Mac host (uses /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/hexa shim → re-routes through docker container). Result: identical 16/16 PASS, offline_fallback=3, wall=37000ms — confirms in-container measurement is canonical.
measurement_setup_notes.secondary_finding: The shim-vs-native ambiguity is itself a latent bridge_health.sh defect when invoked with `docker exec` instead of via the host wrapper. Add to next_actions.

predicted_vs_actual.delta_explanation: uniprot ALSO recovered (predicted FAIL). Prediction assumed the sentinel bug fired whenever a fetch was attempted. Actual semantics: bug only fires when fetch is attempted-and-empty-body (curl-not-found returned empty in pre-curl baseline). With curl present the live UniProt P68871 fetch returns real JSON, populating fields AND letting the sentinel emit ok=true via the live-success branch — bypassing the broken fallback-attempted-but-empty path entirely. The bug still latently exists (it would re-fire if UniProt 5xx'd or returned empty), but is masked while live-fetch succeeds. Net: 9/16 → 16/16 (+7 instead of +6).
predicted_vs_actual.post_curl_pass_count_delta_vs_baseline: +7 PASS (oeis, gw, arxiv, simbad, wikipedia, openalex, uniprot — exactly the previous FAIL set)
predicted_vs_actual.hidden_fallback_unmasking_check: F3 friction (silent fallback masking on the 9 has-fallback bridges) did NOT surface latent regressions this run. All 9 previously-PASSing bridges remained PASS. offline_fallback counter = 3 (unchanged from baseline narrative — likely codata/horizons/cmb or similar emitting fallback substring even on live success). No 429, no schema-drift, no DNS, no TLS errors observed in any bridge stdout.

regression_classes.evidence: All 9 previously-PASS bridges (codata/horizons/cmb/nanograv/icecube/nist_atomic/gaia/lhc/pubchem) remain PASS with same sentinel format and no error markers. simbad live-target fetch succeeded for 4/4 targets in 6s. arxiv pulled fresh entries (latest 2026-04-23). openalex returned 3 entries with live IDs. wikipedia returned 4 refs with all_ok=true.

bridge_health.sh defaults HEXA_BIN to the resolver shim, which is fine when invoked from the Mac host (the shim re-routes back into docker correctly) but fails silently when invoked via `docker exec hexa-exec bash bridge_health.sh` because the shim then attempts to exec the Mac-arch hexa.real binary inside linux. All 16 bridges report ec=126 with no diagnostic. This is a NEW failure mode unlocked by today's container path becoming usable.
regression_classes.suggested_fix: (a) bridge_health.sh auto-detects in-container execution via /.dockerenv presence and switches HEXA_BIN to /usr/local/bin/hexa, OR (b) document HEXA_BIN override convention in tool/bridge_health.sh header. Out-of-scope today (no edits per constraints) — file as omega-bridge-8 candidate.

next_actions: omega-bridge-2 still recommended even though uniprot transiently passes — the sentinel-classification bug is latent and will re-fire on the first UniProt outage / empty-body response. Fix is one-line in tool/uniprot_bridge.hexa (set ok=true source=fallback when fallback fields populate, mirror gaia pattern).
